groups.title: Survey about shopping in Broadbeach
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the form below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Survey about shopping in Broadbeach Name: Martyn $22267$ Today's journey to Broadbeach town centre: used his $22268$. Purpose of today's trip - has visited the $22269$ - looking for a new $22270$ - collecting $22271$ (after repair) Preferred day for shopping: $22272$ Opinions about shopping in the town centre Finds the service in shops is excellent. Thinks there are too many places selling $22273$. Would like more places to buy $22274$. Opinions about new out-of-town Shopping Centre Likes the $22275$ best. Believes the $22276$ is unnecessary.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写受访者的姓氏。对话中，男士先说出全名“Martyn Leigh”，随后在被问及拼写时明确回答“L-E-I-G-H”。虽然调查员误以为是“L-double-E”，但男士纠正了拼写，确认姓氏为“Leigh”。因此，此处应填“Leigh”，且符合专有名词首字母大写的惯例。

题目询问今日前往Broadbeach的交通方式。男士明确表示：“I'm on my motorbike today because I'm going to work later”，说明他今天骑的是摩托车。尽管他平时通常乘公交车（normally I catch the bus），但题干强调“today's journey”，因此正确答案是“motorbike”，对应表格中的“used his motorbike”。
题干要求填写今日已访问的地点。男士回答：“I've just been to the hairdresser”，并解释是为了几天后的面试做准备。该信息直接对应“has visited the...”这一项，且“hairdresser”为单数可数名词，符合“ONE WORD ONLY”的要求。虽然后文提到买西装和取电脑，但只有理发店是已经完成的行程。
男士说明接下来要去买一套西装用于面试：“now I'm on my way to buy a suit that I can wear to the interview”，并补充自己目前没有西装。题干中“looking for a new...”正是对此计划的转述，“suit”作为正式服装，符合语境且为单数名词，完全匹配题目要求。

题干要求填写送修后需取回的物品。男士提到：“I've got to go and pick up my laptop. It broke... they had to order a spare part, but it's ready for collection now”。这里的“pick up”与题干中的“collecting”形成同义替换，“laptop”是唯一被提及送修并准备取回的物品，符合“after repair”的限定。
题目询问偏好的购物日。男士回答：“If I'm free on a Monday, that's when I choose to come into town. The shops are less busy then”，明确指出周一因人少而更受青睐。虽然当天是周六，但他强调偏好取决于空闲时间，而周一才是他主动选择的日子，因此答案为“Monday”。
题干要求填写他认为过多的商品类型。女士提到新开了很多咖啡店，男士回应：“No, there are too many of them”，并认为这些店铺占用了本可用于其他商店的空间。咖啡店（coffee shops）售卖的是“coffee”，因此“places selling coffee”即指咖啡店，答案“coffee”准确对应其不满的对象。
男士表示目前只有一家书店，希望有更多选择：“there's only one place that sells books... It'd be nice to have a choice”。题干“more places to buy...”正是对此愿望的转述，“books”作为可数名词复数，符合语境且是唯一被提及希望增加销售点的商品类别。
在评价新购物中心时，男士提到玻璃屋顶很 impressive，但“My favourite thing is the plants they've put around the building”。题干“likes the... best”直接对应“my favourite thing”，因此答案为“plants”。虽然建筑和屋顶也被提及，但只有植物被明确称为“最喜爱的”。
女士询问是否使用过新购物中心的娱乐设施，如影院，男士回答：“Nah, not yet. I can't see any advantage... because the one in town is actually bigger”。这表明他认为新建的影院并无必要，因为市区的更大更好。题干“believes the... is unnecessary”正对应此观点，故答案为“cinema”。

transcript.en: PART 1
You will hear an interviewer who is doing a survey asking a man some questions about shopping.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一名正在做调查的采访者询问一位男士关于购物的一些问题。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 6.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第1至第6题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 6.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第1至第6题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Good morning. I'm doing a survey of shoppers in Broadbeach. Would you have a few minutes to spare to answer some questions?
transcript.cn: 女士：早上好，我在布罗德比奇对购物者进行调查，您能抽出几分钟时间回答一些问题吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Oh...erm…yes, I guess that would be ok.
transcript.cn: 男士：哦……嗯……是的，我想可以。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Thank you very much. Everybody seems so busy today.
transcript.cn: 女士：非常感谢，今天大家似乎都很忙。

transcript.en: MAN: Well actually I don't have loads of time, so…
transcript.cn: 男士：嗯，实际上我没有太多时间，所以……

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh yes, of course. It really won't take long. Could I start by taking your name please?
transcript.cn: 女士：哦是的，当然，真的不会花太长时间。我能先记录一下您的姓名吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Martyn Leigh. Martyn's with a Y.
transcript.cn: 男士：马丁·利，马丁的拼法带有字母Y。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And is your family name spelt L-double-E?
transcript.cn: 女士：您的姓是拼作L-double-E吗？

transcript.en: MAN: It's L-E-I-G-H.
transcript.cn: 男士：是L-E-I-G-H。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Thank you. We don't actually publish your name or details. It just makes it easier for me to identify people when I look at all the results at the end.
transcript.cn: 女士：谢谢，我们实际上不会公布您的姓名或个人信息，这只是让我在最后查看所有结果时更容易识别人们。

transcript.en: MAN: I see.
transcript.cn: 男士：我明白了。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And can I ask, how did you get into town today?
transcript.cn: 女士：我能问一下，您今天是怎么进城的？

transcript.en: MAN: Well, normally I catch the bus, but I'm on my motorbike today because I'm going to work later.
transcript.cn: 男士：嗯，通常我坐公共汽车，但今天我骑摩托车，因为我待会儿要去工作。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And could you tell me what you're doing this morning, I mean, the reason for your trip into town?
transcript.cn: 女士：您能告诉我您今天早上在做什么，也就是说，您进城的原因？

transcript.en: MAN: I've just been to the hairdresser. You see, I have a job interview at the council in a few days.
transcript.cn: 男士：我刚去了理发店，因为几天后我要参加市政厅的求职面试。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh really! Well, good luck with that.
transcript.cn: 女士：哦，真的！祝您好运。

transcript.en: MAN: Thanks a lot. And now I'm on my way to buy a suit that I can wear to the interview. I don't actually own one at the moment!
transcript.cn: 男士：非常感谢。现在我正在去买一套可以穿去面试的西装，我目前实际上没有西装！

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK. Is that everything you're planning to do in town?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，那是您计划在城里做的全部事情吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yeah. Well, I've got to go and pick up my laptop. It broke a couple of days ago so I took it to the shop to get it fixed. They had to order a spare part, but apparently it's ready for collection now. I can't wait to get it back.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的。嗯，我还得去取我的笔记本电脑。它几天前坏了，所以我把它拿到店里修理，他们必须订购一个备用零件，但显然现在已经可以取了，我迫不及待想把它拿回来。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I'm sure. I hate it when my technology breaks down. One more thing, it's Saturday today – is that when you like to do your shopping?
transcript.cn: 女士：我相信，我讨厌我的技术设备出故障。还有一件事，今天是星期六——这是您喜欢购物的时间吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Well it's more a question of when I'm free. If I'm free on a Monday, that's when I choose to come into town. The shops are less busy then, which I always prefer.
transcript.cn: 男士：嗯，这更多是我什么时候有空的问题。如果我周一有空，那就是我选择进城的时间，那时商店没那么忙，我总是更喜欢那样。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 7 to 10.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余对话之前，你有一些时间查看第7至第10题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 7 to 10.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第7至第10题。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So, I'd like to ask you a few more questions to get your views about shopping in Broadbeach, if that's ok.
transcript.cn: 女士：那么，如果可以的话，我想再问您几个问题，了解您对在布罗德比奇购物的看法。

transcript.en: MAN: Sure. What would you like to know specifically?
transcript.cn: 男士：当然，您具体想了解什么？

transcript.en: WOMAN: What would you say you like best about the shops here?
transcript.cn: 女士：您最喜欢这里商店的什么？

transcript.en: MAN: I'd probably say it's the service you get wherever you go.
transcript.cn: 男士：我可能会说是无论您去哪里都能得到的服务。

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK. And what do you think about the range of shops in Broadbeach?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，您觉得布罗德比奇的商店种类怎么样？

transcript.en: MAN: Oh you can get almost anything you want here.
transcript.cn: 男士：哦，您几乎可以在这里买到任何您想要的东西。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And what about recent changes? What do you like and dislike about them? For example, there are a lot of new coffee shops now, are you enjoying them?
transcript.cn: 女士：那最近的变化呢？您喜欢和不喜欢什么？例如，现在有很多新的咖啡馆，您喜欢它们吗？

transcript.en: MAN: No, there are too many of them. It's a shame as those places could be occupied by other kinds of shops.
transcript.cn: 男士：不，有太多了，很遗憾，那些地方本可以被其他种类的商店占据。

transcript.en: WOMAN: What would you like to see instead?
transcript.cn: 女士：您希望看到什么来代替？

transcript.en: MAN: Well, I think we have enough clothing shops. But there's only one place that sells books at the moment. It'd be nice to have a choice, you know.
transcript.cn: 男士：嗯，我认为我们有足够多的服装店。但目前只有一个地方卖书，如果有更多选择就好了。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Right. And have you been to the new shopping centre outside Broadbeach yet, Martyn?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的。马丁，您去过布罗德比奇郊外的新购物中心了吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, once or twice. It's not that far from where I live.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，去过一两次，离我住的地方不太远。

transcript.en: WOMAN: It's a very modern-looking building, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 女士：这是一栋看起来非常现代的建筑，不是吗？

transcript.en: MAN: Yes, and it's lovely. The glass roof is certainly impressive. But my favourite thing is the plants they've put around the building. They're amazing, and so big already.
transcript.cn: 男士：是的，而且很漂亮，玻璃屋顶确实令人印象深刻。但我最喜欢的是他们在建筑周围种植的植物，它们令人惊叹，而且已经长得很大了。

transcript.en: WOMAN: And what about the entertainment facilities? Have you used any of them yet? Like the new cinema?
transcript.cn: 女士：那娱乐设施呢？您使用过其中的任何设施吗？比如新电影院？

transcript.en: MAN: Nah, not yet. I can't see any advantage in having it there because the one in town is actually bigger.
transcript.cn: 男士：没有，还没有。我看不出在那里建电影院有什么优势，因为城里的那个实际上更大。

transcript.en: WOMAN: OK, well thank you so much for…
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，非常感谢您……

groups.desc:
Questions 11 and 12
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: the Digital Marketing Centre

list.title: B
list.content: the TalkCon Zone

list.title: C
list.content: the Breakout area

list.title: D
list.content: the Business Village

list.title: E
list.content: the Business Connections Zone

collect_option.title: In which TWO areas of the business exhibition did James Craig promote his company last year?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出James Craig去年推广公司的两个区域。原文明确提到，去年他们再次使用了Breakout area（因受欢迎而继续使用），并赞助了Business Connections Zone。这两处均属于去年的推广地点，分别对应选项C和E。虽然他也提到希望今年进入Digital Marketing centre，但这是未来计划，并非去年行为，因此排除A。
本题与第11题共享题干，答案一致。听力中James Craig指出去年继续使用Breakout area，并赞助了Business Connections Zone，这两个区域是他去年实际用于推广的场所。其他选项如Business Village是他第一年使用的，TalkCon Zone仅为设想，均不符合“last year”的时间限定，故正确答案为C和E。

groups.desc:
Questions 13 and 14
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: They are available to all members of exhibiting companies.

list.title: B
list.content: They can be used for both food and clothing.

list.title: C
list.content: They only apply if people spend at least GBP400.

list.title: D
list.content: They can be used by family members.

list.title: E
list.content: The percentage saved is always the same.

collect_option.title: Which TWO facts are given about discounts on popular brands available to exhibitors?

questions.analysis: 题目询问关于参展商可享品牌折扣的两项事实。原文提到该折扣计划“available to every member of your organisation”，对应选项A“对参展公司所有成员开放”；同时折扣适用于“major high street fashion and jewellery outlets, as well as grocery chains”，即涵盖服装和食品，对应选项B。其他选项如固定折扣比例、仅限本人或需消费满额等，原文均未提及。
此题与第13题共用题干，答案相同。听力明确说明折扣适用于组织内所有成员（every member），且覆盖时尚品牌（服装）和杂货连锁（食品），因此A和B正确。选项C错误，因为£400是平均节省金额，非消费门槛；D和E在原文中无依据，故排除。

groups.title: Topics
groups.desc:
Questions 15-20
Which topic will each of the following speakers focus on?
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 15-20.

list.title: A
list.content: Supporting job seekers

list.title: B
list.content: Dealing with personal problems

list.title: C
list.content: Effects of an unexpectedly rapid expansion

list.title: D
list.content: A global range of business experiences

list.title: E
list.content: Coping with financial set-backs

list.title: F
list.content: Developing a company in response to changing markets

list.title: G
list.content: Combining business success with contributions to charities

questions.title: Jim Clowrie
questions.analysis: Jim Clowrie的演讲重点在于其餐饮业务出人意料地迅速扩张——从一家小咖啡馆发展到全球76家餐厅，这正是“意外快速扩张的影响”（选项C）。原文用“Rather to his surprise this became a bit of a sensation”强调其始料未及，完全契合C项描述，而非单纯谈创业或财务问题。

questions.title: David France
questions.analysis: David France不仅商业成功，还“regularly donates to organisations helping those in need”，体现其将商业成就与慈善贡献结合，对应选项G。虽然他少年创业的经历突出，但题目问的是演讲重点，原文明确指出他“isn’t just interested in making money”，强调慈善部分，因此G比单纯讲创业或克服困难更准确。

questions.title: Oliver Stanton
questions.analysis: Oliver Stanton的演讲聚焦于他在马来西亚、日本、香港等地的跨国商业经历，并总结“what he discovered... about business practices in different cultures”，这直接对应选项D“全球范围的商业经验”。其他选项如应对挫折或市场变化虽可能相关，但原文未强调这些方面，D最全面概括其主题。

questions.title: Francesca Heptonstall
questions.analysis: Francesca Heptonstall“passionate about helping people gain employment”并组织年度招聘会，因此她的演讲将集中于此，对应选项A“支持求职者”。尽管她也涉足科技和Deal网站，但原文明确说“she’ll be concentrating on this”，即就业支持是本次演讲核心，排除其他干扰项。

questions.title: Salman Khan
questions.analysis: Salman Khan将QBF Enterprises从传统出版转型为数字营销巨头，演讲主题是“the need for flexibility to cope with new directions in today’s business world”，这正体现了“根据市场变化发展公司”（选项F）。F项中的“changing markets”与原文“new directions”形成同义替换，准确反映其核心观点。

questions.title: Annie Craven
questions.analysis: Annie Craven的环节关注“obstacles in people’s own lives, not just in business”，并探讨如何克服这些障碍，无论处于事业哪个阶段。这明显指向个人生活中的问题，对应选项B“处理个人问题”。虽然涉及商业，但原文强调“not just in business”，说明重点在更广泛的个人层面，故B最贴切。

transcript.en: PART 2
You will hear a podcast about a large business exhibition which is held every year in London.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一档关于每年在伦敦举办的大型商业展览的播客。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 14.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第11至第14题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 14.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第11至第14题。

transcript.en: Hello and welcome to this podcast telling you about our annual business exhibition. This year, as always, it will be full of the very best speakers, features and innovations.
transcript.cn: 你好，欢迎收听这个关于我们年度商业展览的播客。今年，一如既往，将充满最优秀的演讲者、特色活动和创新内容。

transcript.en: Let me start by reading some feedback from James Craig, who's exhibited with us for the past two years.
transcript.cn: 让我先读一些詹姆斯·克雷格的反馈，他在过去两年里一直与我们一起参展。

transcript.en: James says, 'I'm director of a company called TalkCon, which is an office phone system.
transcript.cn: 詹姆斯说：「我是一家名为TalkCon的公司的总监，这是一个办公室电话系统。

transcript.en: The first year we exhibited, we had a stand in the Business Village, where we made a lot of useful contacts.
transcript.cn: 我们第一年参展时，在商业村有一个摊位，我们建立了很多有用的联系。

transcript.en: We also used the Breakout area where people could sit down and relax, and try out our products, and due to popular request we used that again last year.
transcript.cn: 我们还使用了休息区，人们可以在那里坐下放松，并试用我们的产品，由于广受欢迎，去年我们再次使用了那个区域。

transcript.en: Last year we also sponsored the innovative Business Connections Zone where people could leave their contact details on a board to contact other companies, and that also effectively raised our corporate profile.
transcript.cn: 去年我们还赞助了创新的商业联系区，人们可以在那里在板上留下联系方式与其他公司联系，这也有效地提升了我们的企业形象。

transcript.en: We're hoping that in the coming exhibition we'll also have a presence in the Digital Marketing centre, which is clearly a key area for our company. And also maybe have our own Talkcon Zone, at some stage.'
transcript.cn: 我们希望在即将举行的展览中，我们也能在数字营销中心展示，这显然是我们公司的关键领域，也许在某个阶段还有我们自己的TalkCon专区。」

transcript.en: The exhibition's open from 8am to 11pm and there's a range of special events for you to enjoy in the evening.
transcript.cn: 展览从早上8点开放到晚上11点，晚上有各种特别活动供您享用。

transcript.en: As well as a unique chance to publicise your company, businesses who exhibit with us can claim discounts on a number of popular brands, including major high street fashion and jewellery outlets, as well as grocery chains.
transcript.cn: 除了宣传您公司的独特机会外，与我们一起参展的企业还可以获得多个热门品牌的折扣，包括主要的高街时尚和珠宝店，以及连锁超市。

transcript.en: Discounts available range from 3.5% up to an amazing 15%, so you can save a lot of money.
transcript.cn: 可享受的折扣从3.5%到惊人的15%不等，因此您可以节省很多钱。

transcript.en: In fact, the average saving made by each exhibitor came to over £400! The scheme is available to every member of your organisation so this is a benefit that really does have something for you all.
transcript.cn: 事实上，每位参展商的平均节省超过400英镑！该计划对您组织的每位成员都适用，因此这是一项真正对大家都有益的福利。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the podcast, you have some time to look at questions 15 to 20.
transcript.cn: 在你听播客剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第15至第20题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 15 to 20.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第15至第20题。

transcript.en: Now let me tell you about some of our keynote speakers for this year's exhibition.
transcript.cn: 现在让我告诉您今年展览的一些主题演讲嘉宾。

transcript.en: Jim Clowrie started off selling vegetables from a small plot, then opened a small café.
transcript.cn: 吉姆·克劳里从一小块地种菜开始，然后开了一家小咖啡馆。

transcript.en: Rather to his surprise this became a bit of a sensation, and in a remarkably short time he had opened an amazing 76 restaurants worldwide. He'll be telling you how it all happened, and how it changed his life.
transcript.cn: 令他惊讶的是，这引起了一些轰动，在非常短的时间内，他在全球开了惊人的76家餐厅。他将告诉您这一切是如何发生的，以及它如何改变了他的生活。

transcript.en: David France will be giving an inspirational talk about how as a business-minded teenager he managed to set up his own company, and even to be the first person under 18 to get a business bank account.
transcript.cn: 大卫·法兰斯将发表一场励志演讲，讲述他作为一个具有商业头脑的青少年如何成功创立自己的公司，甚至成为第一个在18岁以下获得商业银行账户的人。

transcript.en: David's company has gone from strength to strength but he isn't just interested in making money – he also makes regular donations to organisations helping those in need both in the UK and elsewhere in the world.
transcript.cn: 大卫的公司日益壮大，但他不仅仅对赚钱感兴趣——他还定期向英国和世界其他地方帮助有需要的人的组织捐款。

transcript.en: Oliver Stanton was born and educated in the UK, and then went to Malaysia where he set up a rubber-wood furniture business.
transcript.cn: 奥利弗·斯坦顿在英国出生和受教育，然后前往马来西亚在那里建立了一家橡木家具业务。

transcript.en: He then built log houses in the mountains in Japan before moving to Hong Kong to set up a digital marketing business. After that he moved back to the UK with his wife Saiphin, and together they opened a Thai café.
transcript.cn: 然后他在日本山区建造了木屋，之后移居香港建立了一家数字营销业务。之后他与妻子赛平回到英国，一起开了一家泰国咖啡馆。

transcript.en: He'll be telling you about what he discovered during his travels around the world about business practices in different cultures.
transcript.cn: 他将告诉您他在世界各地旅行中发现的关于不同文化中商业实践的内容。

transcript.en: Francesca Heptonstall is a broadcaster and businesswoman who's known for her down-to-earth attitude.
transcript.cn: 弗朗西斯卡·赫普顿斯托尔是一位以脚踏实地的态度著称的广播主持人和女商人。

transcript.en: After winning a top job in a TV contest, she launched a number of online deal sites.
transcript.cn: 在赢得一场电视比赛中的顶级职位后，她创办了多个在线交易网站。

transcript.en: Her main business interest is in technology, but she's also passionate about helping people gain employment and organises an annual jobs fair, so she'll be concentrating on this in her talk.
transcript.cn: 她主要的商业兴趣在于技术，但她也热衷于帮助人们就业并组织年度就业博览会，因此她的演讲将集中在这方面。

transcript.en: Salman Khan is the Chief Executive of QBF Enterprises, and has turned around this business from a major publishing business to one of the UK's largest digital marketing service providers.
transcript.cn: 萨尔曼·汗是QBF企业的首席执行官，他将这个企业从一家主要出版商转变为英国最大的数字营销服务提供商之一。

transcript.en: He'll be talking about the need for flexibility to cope with new directions in today's business world.
transcript.cn: 他将谈论在当今商业世界中应对新方向的灵活性需求。

transcript.en: Finally, Annie Craven is a consultant and coach who works with people to create lasting changes in their business and life.
transcript.cn: 最后，安妮·克雷文是一位顾问和教练，她与人们合作为他们的商业和生活创造持久的变化。

transcript.en: Her session will offer an interactive look at different obstacles in people's own lives, not just in business. She also looks at how you can overcome them, whether you're starting out in business or at the top of your game.
transcript.cn: 她的课程将以互动方式探讨人们生活中的不同障碍，不仅仅是在商业方面，她还探讨了无论您是刚起步还是处于事业巅峰，如何克服这些障碍。

transcript.en: Now let me…
transcript.cn: 现在让我……

groups.title: Presentation on houses of the future
groups.desc:
Questions 21-23
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Which aspect of their presentation are Mia and Leo both concerned about?
questions.analysis: 题目问Mia和Leo共同担心的方面。Mia一开始表示担忧是否需要提出创意建议，而Leo明确回应说重点在于‘reporting and evaluating possible developments’，并强调必须引用具体案例（specific cases）来支撑观点，这说明他们关注的是能否找到合适的例子，而非原创性或截止日期。因此正确选项是B（finding suitable examples）。

questions.title: The students decide to focus their assignment on housing for
questions.analysis: 对话中Leo提议‘housing for different generations living together’，Mia虽考虑过独居或老年住房，但最终认为跨代同住‘might be more interesting – let's go with that’。‘不同世代同住’即指家庭群体（family groups），而非仅限老人或单身人士。因此正确答案是A（family groups）。

questions.title: The students agree that demand for accommodation in urban areas should be met by

options.title: C
options.content: developing creative ideas for smaller homes.

questions.analysis: 针对城市住房需求，Mia提出‘design multi-storey apartment blocks’作为解决方案，Leo随即附和‘Building up rather than out, yes’，明确支持建造高层建筑。虽然Leo曾提到改造商业建筑（A项），Mia也提过小户型创意（C项），但两人最终达成一致的是建高楼，故选B（constructing tall buildings）。

groups.title: Opinions
groups.desc:
Questions 24-30
What opinion is given about each of the following developments?
Choose SEVEN answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-I, next to Questions 24-30.

list.title: A
list.content: This could cause unnecessary anxiety.

list.title: B
list.content: This would be especially beneficial for city residents.

list.title: C
list.content: This would be challenging for young people.

list.title: D
list.content: This would have environmental benefits.

list.title: E
list.content: This could encourage creativity.

list.title: F
list.content: This could lead to social problems.

list.title: G
list.content: This could enable retired people to share a project.

list.title: H
list.content: This would help some people but cause problems for others.

list.title: I
list.content: This would suit both existing and new members of a household.

questions.title: use of roof space for gardens
questions.analysis: 关于屋顶花园，Mia指出即使郊区平房也可实施，但‘it would greatly improve how you feel if you live in an urban high-rise’，强调对城市高楼居民的特别益处。选项B‘especially beneficial for city residents’正是对此的同义转述，因此选B。

questions.title: shared working spaces
questions.analysis: 谈及共享工作空间时，Leo提到人们可与不同职业者互动，‘That’s often how new ideas get generated’，暗示这种交流能激发创意。选项E‘could encourage creativity’准确概括了这一观点，因此选E。

questions.title: moveable internal walls
questions.analysis: Mia介绍可移动内墙设计后，Leo举例说明其适用场景：子女工作后仍与父母同住，或老人搬入家庭，都能拥有专属空间。这表明该设计能适应既有成员和新加入成员的需求，与选项I‘suit both existing and new members of a household’完全对应。

questions.title: smart mirrors in bathrooms
questions.analysis: Mia提到智能镜子会自动识别疾病征兆并联系医生，但她和Leo都认为‘People might worry about conditions which aren't serious at all’，即可能引发不必要的焦虑。选项A‘could cause unnecessary anxiety’直接反映这一担忧，因此选A。

questions.title: bike sheds with charging points
questions.analysis: Leo提议设置带充电桩的自行车棚，Mia回应说这会鼓励更多人骑车而非开车，并强调‘Much better for the planet’，明确指向环保效益。选项D‘would have environmental benefits’正是对此的总结，故选D。

questions.title: restriction of cars to certain areas
questions.analysis: Leo认为限制车辆进入住宅区对有孩子的家庭很好，但Mia立即指出这对行动不便的老人或残障人士不利。双方共识是该措施‘help some people but cause problems for others’，与选项H完全吻合，因此选H。

questions.title: communal vegetable plots
questions.analysis: Mia提到社区菜园能让邻居共同决定种植内容，并特别指出‘That’d be a great way for older people to get to know one another, especially if they're no longer going out to work’，即退休人士可通过该项目互动合作。选项G‘enable retired people to share a project’准确概括此意，故选G。

transcript.en: PART 3
You will hear two architecture students, called Mia and Leo, discussing their presentation on houses of the future.
transcript.cn: 你将听到两名建筑学学生米娅和利奥讨论他们关于未来住宅的报告。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 23.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第21至第23题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 23.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第23题。

transcript.en: MIA: You know that joint presentation we've got to do this semester, Leo?
transcript.cn: 米娅：利奥，你知道我们这学期必须做的那个联合报告吗？

transcript.en: LEO: On houses of the future?
transcript.cn: 利奥：关于未来的房屋？

transcript.en: MIA: That's right. I'm a bit concerned – are we meant to come up with creative new suggestions for these houses?
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，我有点担心——我们应该为这些房屋提出创新的新建议吗？

transcript.en: LEO: I don't think so. It's more a matter of reporting and evaluating possible developments. But we mustn't be too general, we've got to support our points by referring to specific cases. So that'll need a lot of work.
transcript.cn: 利奥：我不这么认为，更多的是报告和评估可能的发展。但我们不能太笼统，我们必须通过引用具体案例来支持我们的观点，所以这需要大量工作。

transcript.en: MIA: I'm afraid so. When's it got to be done by?
transcript.cn: 米娅：恐怕是的，必须什么时候完成？

transcript.en: LEO: In about 6 weeks, so that's not too much of a rush.
transcript.cn: 利奥：大约6周后，所以不是太紧迫。

transcript.en: MIA: Good. We'd better decide now what type of housing we're going to focus on.
transcript.cn: 米娅：好的，我们最好现在决定要关注什么类型的住房。

transcript.en: LEO: How about housing for different generations living together?
transcript.cn: 利奥：不同世代共同生活的住房怎么样？

transcript.en: MIA: We could do. Or accommodation for one person?
transcript.cn: 米娅：我们可以这样做，或者一个人居住的住所？

transcript.en: LEO: I think someone else is doing that. I was wondering about housing for the elderly? That's likely to become more important.
transcript.cn: 利奥：我认为其他人正在做这个，我在考虑老年人住房，这可能会变得更加重要。

transcript.en: MIA: Yeah that's true. But I think your suggestion about intergenerational living might be more interesting – let's go with that.
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，确实如此。但我认为你关于代际共同生活的建议可能更有趣——我们就选那个吧。

transcript.en: LEO: OK. Now I think the future demand is mainly going to be for accommodation in urban areas. So one way of meeting that demand might be to use existing commercial buildings and adapt them to form accommodation…
transcript.cn: 利奥：好的，我认为未来的需求主要将是城市地区的住房。所以满足这一需求的一种方式可能是利用现有的商业建筑并将其改造成住宅……

transcript.en: MIA: …or come up with original ways of organising space so that people can live in smaller homes. But I think the solution is to design multi-storey apartment blocks.
transcript.cn: 米娅：……或者提出组织空间的新方式，使人们可以住在更小的房子里。但我认为解决方案是设计多层公寓楼。

transcript.en: LEO: Building up rather than out, yes.
transcript.cn: 利奥：向上建而不是向外扩展，是的。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 24 to 30.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间查看第24至第30题。

transcript.en: MIA: Let's think of some specific developments for houses of the future.
transcript.cn: 米娅：让我们考虑一些未来房屋的具体发展。

transcript.en: LEO: OK. How about increased use of roof space on high-rise buildings for gardens.
transcript.cn: 利奥：好的，如何增加高层建筑屋顶空间用于花园？

transcript.en: MIA: Yes. In fact it doesn't have to be high-rise, you can do it on a one-storey building in a suburb, but it would greatly improve how you feel if you live in an urban high-rise.
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，事实上不必是高层建筑，您可以在郊区的单层建筑上这样做，但如果您住在城市高层建筑中，这将大大改善您的感受。

transcript.en: LEO: Especially if you don't have a balcony.
transcript.cn: 利奥：特别是如果您没有阳台的话。

transcript.en: MIA: Yes. I think homes of the future will all need access to a shared working space, somewhere in the same building or group of houses, where people can go and work instead of just having a laptop on the kitchen table.
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，我认为未来的家庭都需要能够使用共享工作空间，在同一栋建筑或一组房屋中的某个地方，人们可以去那里工作，而不仅仅是把笔记本电脑放在厨房桌子上。

transcript.en: LEO: Yes, so they aren't having to travel to an office but can still interact with others. That's often how new ideas get generated – by chatting to someone from a different profession.
transcript.cn: 利奥：是的，这样他们就不必去办公室，但仍然可以与他人互动。这通常是新想法产生的方式——通过与来自不同职业的人交流。

transcript.en: MIA: Yeah. I read about a type of design where the internal walls of an apartment are moveable, so the space can be adapted over time as people's needs change.
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，我读到过一种设计，其中公寓的内墙是可移动的，这样空间可以随着时间推移根据人们需求的变化而调整。

transcript.en: LEO: Like when children leave school and start working but still continue to live with their parents for many years? Or when an elderly relative moves in with the family… it would mean they could still have their own space, specially designed for their needs.
transcript.cn: 利奥：就像当孩子们离开学校开始工作，但仍然继续与父母住在一起多年？或者当年长的亲戚搬来与家人住在一起……这意味着他们仍然可以拥有自己的空间，专门为他们的需求而设计。

transcript.en: MIA: Yes. Have you heard about those smart bathroom mirrors which can monitor people's health? They recognise signs of illness and contact a doctor automatically?
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，你听说过那些可以监测人们健康状况的智能浴室镜子吗？它们可以识别疾病迹象并自动联系医生？

transcript.en: LEO: Hmm, not so sure they're a good idea.
transcript.cn: 利奥：嗯，我不太确定这是个好主意。

transcript.en: MIA: Nor am I. People might worry about conditions which aren't serious at all.
transcript.cn: 米娅：我也不确定，人们可能会担心那些根本不严重的情况。

transcript.en: LEO: What about transport? Wouldn't it be good if there were bike sheds with charging points, so people could store their electric bikes securely and charge them up at the same time.
transcript.cn: 利奥：那交通呢？如果有带充电点的自行车棚不是很好吗，这样人们可以安全地存放他们的电动自行车并同时为它们充电。

transcript.en: MIA: Yes. That would encourage more people to cycle, instead of using their car. Much better for the planet.
transcript.cn: 米娅：是的，这将鼓励更多人骑自行车而不是开车，对地球更好。

transcript.en: LEO: I read about one housing development where cars had to be left just outside it, so the centre was all a pedestrianised area. Great for families with children.
transcript.cn: 利奥：我读到过一个住宅开发项目，在那里汽车必须停在外面，所以中心是全步行区，对有孩子的家庭很好。

transcript.en: MIA: Maybe. But what if you're disabled or elderly, and can't walk far? It wouldn't be so good for people like that.
transcript.cn: 米娅：也许吧。但如果您有残疾或年老，走不了多远怎么办？对那样的人来说就不那么好了。

transcript.en: LEO: No.
transcript.cn: 利奥：是的。

transcript.en: MIA: I saw a scheme for communal vegetable plots, where neighbours could decide what to grow together. That'd be a great way for older people to get to know one another, especially if they're no longer going out to work.
transcript.cn: 米娅：我看到了一个共用菜地的方案，邻居们可以一起决定种什么。这对老年人来说是一个很好的相互认识的方式，特别是如果他们不再外出工作的话。

transcript.en: LEO: Yes, doing something together's always more enjoyable, isn't it? Do you think…
transcript.cn: 利奥：是的，一起做某事总是更愉快的，不是吗？你认为……

groups.title: Music therapy for surgical patients
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Background Surgery impacts patients because they may experience discomfort or unwelcome changes to their $22297$. Current post-surgical strategies focus mainly on pain relief. Recent research A study reviewed data from about 100 $22298$ and found that listening to music improved hospital patients' sense of wellbeing reduced the length of their stay. The patients in the study all listened to music with a $22299$ effect. The music was mostly played through music $22300$. Patients reported an absence or low levels of $22301$. Medical records confirmed that patients who were played music in hospital needed less $22302$ than those who were not played music. The best results were achieved when patients were played music while they were $22303$. The study concluded that playing music was effective because it served as a $22304$. The researchers recommend playing either music or sounds from $22305$ to all surgical patients. A future study will investigate the best $22306$ for the music.

questions.analysis: 题干提到手术会对患者的 routine（日常作息）造成干扰，对应原文第8句 'having their routine disturbed'。虽然原文用的是 'disturbed'，但题干将其转化为名词 'routine' 填空，符合‘一个词’的要求。此处需注意题干中 'unwelcome changes to their...' 的结构提示应填名词，而 'routine' 正是被干扰的对象，逻辑严密且无其他干扰项。
题干要求填写研究回顾的数据来源数量单位，原文第12句明确指出 'nearly a hundred trials'，其中 'trials'（试验）与题干 'data from about 100...' 完全对应。尽管上下文提到七千名患者，但题干聚焦的是研究数量而非人数，因此 'trials' 是唯一准确答案，且符合复数名词的语法要求。
题干询问音乐具有的效果，原文第16句提到 'the chosen music had a calming quality'，其中 'calming'（舒缓的）被题干转换为 'calming effect'。虽然原文用 'quality'，但题干要求填形容词修饰 'effect'，因此只需提取 'calming'。该词直接描述音乐的共同特征，且前后无其他情绪类词汇干扰，定位清晰。
题干问音乐主要通过什么播放，原文第18句明确说明 'the mode of delivery was by what are known as music pillows'，即“音乐枕”。虽前一句提到耳机（headphones），但 'more often though' 表明音乐枕是更常见的播放方式，因此 'pillows' 是正确答案。注意题干要求复数形式，与原文一致。
题干要求填写患者报告缺失或低水平的感受，原文第20句指出患者 'had no feelings of anxiety at any point, or those feelings were only slight'，直接对应 'anxiety'（焦虑）。虽然上下文提到疼痛和不适，但此处特指听音乐后的心理状态，且 'anxiety' 是不可数名词，符合‘一个词’的要求。
题干询问听音乐患者所需减少的医疗手段，原文第23句明确说 'didn't need as much medication to ease their pain'，因此答案为 'medication'（药物）。尽管上下文多次提到疼痛管理，但此处特指药物使用量的减少，且 'medication' 为不可数名词，完全匹配题干 'needed less...' 的结构。

题干问何时效果最佳，原文第25句对比指出 'it even worked under general anaesthetic, although the positive effects were greater when patients were awake'，因此 'awake'（清醒的）是正确答案。虽然麻醉状态下也有效，但题干强调 'best results'，故排除 'anaesthetised' 等干扰可能。
题干问音乐起效的原因，原文第27句明确结论 'it worked on patients by distraction'，即通过“分散注意力”起作用。题干将 'by distraction' 转换为 'served as a distraction'，因此填 'distraction'。该词准确概括了研究者的解释机制，且前后无其他理论干扰。
题干建议播放音乐或来自何处的声音，原文第29句提到 'recordings taken from nature can be just as good as music'，因此答案为 'nature'（自然）。虽然录音内容未具体说明，但 'from nature' 是固定搭配，且题干明确要求 'sounds from...'，逻辑和词汇均精准对应。
题干问未来研究将调查音乐的最佳什么，原文第31句指出 'focus their next research on the most appropriate volume to play the music at'，因此答案为 'volume'（音量）。尽管前文讨论类型、时机等，但此处明确转向音量这一新变量，且 'volume' 符合‘一个词’的要求。

transcript.en: PART 4
You will hear part of a lecture to medical students about playing music to patients who are in hospital for surgery.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一段面向医学生的讲座，内容是给住院接受手术的患者播放音乐。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Today's lecture is about studies that look at how music therapy can be used to help patients who undergo surgery.
transcript.cn: 今天的讲座是关于研究如何利用音乐治疗来帮助接受手术的患者。

transcript.en: Now, most people undergo a surgical procedure at some point in their lives. And more than fifty-one million operations are performed annually in the USA.
transcript.cn: 现在，大多数人在生命中的某个时刻都会接受手术，美国每年进行超过5100万次手术。

transcript.en: But there's no escaping the fact that most patients feel uncomfortable following surgery. They may have difficulty mobilising, and even sitting up in bed can feel too much.
transcript.cn: 但无法回避的事实是，大多数患者在手术后感到不适。他们可能在行动方面有困难，甚至坐在床上都可能感觉很吃力。

transcript.en: They may also be negatively affected by having their routine disturbed, you know, like if they can't do the things they normally do, or have to devote a lot of time to appointments such as physiotherapy, to aid their recovery.
transcript.cn: 他们也可能因为日常规律被打乱而受到负面影响，比如如果他们不能做他们通常做的事情，或者必须花大量时间参加物理治疗等预约来帮助康复。

transcript.en: Currently, the main strategy for improving recovery is medication to control pain, and this can be administered to patients in the short, medium or longer term, depending on the extent of their surgery.
transcript.cn: 目前，改善康复的主要策略是用药物控制疼痛，这可以根据手术的程度在短期、中期或较长期内给予患者。

transcript.en: But music is still not an everyday part of the post-surgical phase, despite a wealth of relevant studies supporting its potential in recovery.
transcript.cn: 但音乐仍然不是术后阶段的日常部分，尽管有大量相关研究支持其在康复中的潜力。

transcript.en: Earlier this year, a research team set out to assess all the available evidence so that they could highlight the potential for music in surgical recovery.
transcript.cn: 今年早些时候，一个研究团队着手评估所有可用的证据，以便他们能够突出音乐在手术康复中的潜力。

transcript.en: They identified nearly a hundred trials involving a total of seven thousand patients who were played recorded music as part of their post-operative care.
transcript.cn: 他们确定了近100项试验，涉及总共7000名患者，这些患者在术后护理中接受了录制音乐的播放。

transcript.en: The researchers then looked at what impact the music had on the patients.
transcript.cn: 研究人员随后研究了音乐对患者的影响。

transcript.en: They discovered that patients who had been played music reported feeling happier and more satisfied in the post-operative phase, and the length of their stay was shorter than for patients who had not listened to any music.
transcript.cn: 他们发现，接受音乐播放的患者报告说在术后阶段感到更快乐、更满意，而且他们的住院时间比没有听音乐的患者更短。

transcript.en: The researchers also explored the patients' choice of music, and their findings showed that a wide variety of music styles was evident.
transcript.cn: 研究人员还探索了患者的音乐选择，他们的发现表明各种各样的音乐风格都有。

transcript.en: However, a common factor was that the chosen music had a calming quality.
transcript.cn: 然而，一个共同因素是所选的音乐具有平静的特质。

transcript.en: Some of the patients listened to music with headphones, but it was quiet enough not to prevent them from being able to communicate with nurses and other staff.
transcript.cn: 一些患者戴着耳机听音乐，但音量足够低，不会妨碍他们与护士和其他工作人员交流。

transcript.en: More often though, the mode of delivery was by what are known as music pillows. These broadcast sound that is only audible to the person lying on them.
transcript.cn: 不过，更常见的传送方式是所谓的音乐枕。这些枕头播放的声音只有躺在上面的人才能听到。

transcript.en: The research involved testing music before, during or after operations or a combination. Some patients listened to the music just once a day, while others had several episodes a day.
transcript.cn: 研究涉及在手术前、手术中或手术后或两者结合的情况下测试音乐。一些患者每天只听一次音乐，而另一些患者每天听几次。

transcript.en: When asked to report their experience of listening to music, surgical patients said that either they had no feelings of anxiety at any point, or those feelings were only slight.
transcript.cn: 当被要求报告听音乐的体验时，手术患者说他们要么在任何时候都没有焦虑感，要么这些感觉只是轻微的。

transcript.en: Patients who hadn't listened to music, on the other hand, reported higher levels of dissatisfaction.
transcript.cn: 另一方面，没有听音乐的患者报告了更高程度的不满。

transcript.en: This feedback from all of the patients was then cross-checked against their medical notes.
transcript.cn: 然后，所有患者的反馈与他们的医疗记录进行了交叉核对。

transcript.en: And in every case, those who were given music to listen to didn't need as much medication to ease their pain as those patients who weren't played music.
transcript.cn: 在每种情况下，那些被给予音乐听的患者不需要像没有播放音乐的患者那么多的药物来缓解疼痛。

transcript.en: The type of music, patient choice and timing, before, during or after the surgery didn't make much difference.
transcript.cn: 音乐的类型、患者的选择以及手术前、手术中或手术后的时间安排没有太大差别。

transcript.en: And it even worked when patients were played music under general anaesthetic, although the positive effects were greater when patients were awake.
transcript.cn: 而且当患者在全身麻醉下播放音乐时也有效果，尽管当患者清醒时正面效果更大。

transcript.en: All the evidence suggests that music has a positive effect on post-operative patients, but it's not entirely clear how or why this is the case.
transcript.cn: 所有证据表明音乐对术后患者有积极影响，但尚不完全清楚这是如何以及为什么发生的。

transcript.en: A lot of people listen to music in daily life as a way to relax and forget their problems, but the researchers came to the conclusion that it worked on patients by distraction. I suppose it was something familiar and gave them something they could control.
transcript.cn: 很多人在日常生活中听音乐作为放松和忘记烦恼的方式，但研究人员得出结论，它通过分散注意力对患者起作用。我想这是熟悉的东西，给了他们一些可以控制的事情。

transcript.en: The researchers say there is now sufficient research to demonstrate that music should be available to all patients undergoing operations.
transcript.cn: 研究人员说，现在有足够的研究证明音乐应该对所有接受手术的患者可用。

transcript.en: They say patients should be able to choose what they'd like to listen to, and if they prefer, recordings taken from nature can be just as good as music.
transcript.cn: 他们说患者应该能够选择他们想听什么，如果他们愿意，来自大自然的录音可以和音乐一样好。

transcript.en: Surgical teams may prefer patients to listen to music before the procedure or as soon as they arrive back onto the ward.
transcript.cn: 手术团队可能更希望患者在手术前或回到病房后尽快听音乐。

transcript.en: Clearly, there's more to learn about this area, and the team now plan to focus their next research on the most appropriate volume to play the music at.
transcript.cn: 显然，在这一领域还有更多东西需要学习，该团队现在计划将下一步研究集中在播放音乐的最合适音量上。

transcript.en: And I look forward to reading the results of their study. It will be interesting to see …
transcript.cn: 我期待阅读他们研究的结果。看看……将会很有趣。
